text.Rotherham: And the, sin-bearing bullock, and the sin-bearing goat whose blood was brought in to make a propitiatory-covering in the holy place, shall be carried forth outside the camp,—and they shall burn up, in fire, their skins and their flesh, and their dung;

text.NRSV: And the one who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, and afterward may come into the camp.
text.Rotherham: and he that hath burned them, shall wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water,—and, after that, shall he come into the camp.

text.NRSV: This shall be a statute to you forever: In the seventh month, on the tenth day of the month, you shall deny yourselves, and shall do no work, neither the citizen nor the alien who resides among you.
text.Rotherham: And it shall become unto you, a statute age-abiding,—In the seventh month on the tenth of the month, Shall ye humble you souls And, no work, shall ye do, The home-born, Or the sojourner that sojourneth in your midst;

text.NRSV: For on this day atonement shall be made for you, to cleanse you; from all your sins you shall be clean before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: For on this day, shall a propitiatory-covering, be put over you, to cleanse you,—From all your sins before Yahweh, shall ye be clean.

text.NRSV: It is a sabbath of complete rest to you, and you shall deny yourselves; it is a statute forever.
text.Rotherham: A sabbath of sacred rest, it is unto you, Therefore shall ye humble your souls, A statute age-abiding.

text.NRSV: The priest who is anointed and consecrated as priest in his father's place shall make atonement, wearing the linen vestments, the holy vestments.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall the priest who shall be anointed, and installed, to minister as priest in the stead of his father make propitiation,—So then he shall put on the linen garments the holy garments;

text.NRSV: He shall make atonement for the sanctuary, and he shall make atonement for the tent of meeting and for the altar, and he shall make atonement for the priests and for all the people of the assembly.
text.Rotherham: And make propitiation for the holy sanctuary, And for the tent of meeting, and for the altar, shall he make propitiation,—Over the priests also and over all the people of the convocation, shall he put a covering by propitiation.

text.NRSV: This shall be an everlasting statute for you, to make atonement for the people of Israel once in the year for all their sins. And Moses did as the Lord had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: So shall this become unto you an age-abiding statute,—to put a propitiatory-covering over the sons of Israel because of all their sins, Once in the year. And he did, As Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:—

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and his sons and to all the people of Israel and say to them: This is what the Lord has commanded.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons and unto all the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded, saying:

text.NRSV: If anyone of the house of Israel slaughters an ox or a lamb or a goat in the camp, or slaughters it outside the camp,
text.Rotherham: What man soever, there be of the house of Israel, who slayeth an ox or lamb or goat, in the camp,—or who slayeth it outside the camp;

text.NRSV: and does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting, to present it as an offering to the Lord before the tabernacle of the Lord, he shall be held guilty of bloodshed; he has shed blood, and he shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: and, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, bringeth it not in, to present it as an oblation unto Yahweh, before the habitation of Yahweh, blood, shall be imputed to that man—blood, hath he shed, therefore shall that man be cut off from the midst of his people:

text.NRSV: This is in order that the people of Israel may bring their sacrifices that they offer in the open field, that they may bring them to the Lord, to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and offer them as sacrifices of well-being to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: to the end that the sons of Israel may bring in their sacrifices which they are offering upon the face of the field, that they may bring them in unto Yahweh—unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, unto the priest,—and that so as peace-offerings unto Yahweh, they may offer them.

text.NRSV: The priest shall dash the blood against the altar of the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and turn the fat into smoke as a pleasing odor to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest dash the blood against the altar of Yahweh, at the entrance of the tent of meeting,—and shall make a perfume of the fat, as a satisfying odour unto Yahweh;

text.NRSV: so that they may no longer offer their sacrifices for goat-demons, to whom they prostitute themselves. This shall be a statute forever to them throughout their generations.
text.Rotherham: so shall they no more offer their sacrifices unto demons after whom they are unchastely going away,—a statute age-abiding, shall this be to them unto their generations.

text.NRSV: And say to them further: Anyone of the house of Israel or of the aliens who reside among them who offers a burnt offering or sacrifice,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, unto them, shalt thou say: What man soever, there may be of the house of Israel, or of the sojourners that sojourn in their midst,—who causeth to go up an ascending-offering, or a sacrifice;

text.NRSV: and does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting, to sacrifice it to the Lord, shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: and unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, doth not bring it in, to offer it unto Yahweh, then shall that man be cut off from among his kinsfolk.

text.NRSV: If anyone of the house of Israel or of the aliens who reside among them eats any blood, I will set my face against that person who eats blood, and will cut that person off from the people.
text.Rotherham: And, what man soever, there may be of the house of Israel, or of the sojourners that sojourn in their midst, that partaketh of any manner of blood, then will I set my face against the person that partaketh of the blood, and will cut him off from the midst of his people.

text.NRSV: For the life of the flesh is in the blood; and I have given it to you for making atonement for your lives on the altar; for, as life, it is the blood that makes atonement.
text.Rotherham: For as for the life of the flesh, in the blood, it is, therefore have, I, given it unto you upon the altar, to put a propitiatory-covering over your lives,—for the blood, it is, which, by virtue of the life, maketh propitiation.

text.NRSV: Therefore I have said to the people of Israel: No person among you shall eat blood, nor shall any alien who resides among you eat blood.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, have I said unto the sons of Israel, Not a person from among you shall partake of blood,—Even the sojourner that sojourneth in your midst, shall not partake of blood.

text.NRSV: And anyone of the people of Israel, or of the aliens who reside among them, who hunts down an animal or bird that may be eaten shall pour out its blood and cover it with earth.
text.Rotherham: And, what man soever, there may be of the sons of Israel, or of the sojourners that sojourn in their midst, who taketh by hunting any wild-beast or bird that may be eaten, then shall he pour out the blood thereof, and cover it with dust;

text.NRSV: For the life of every creature—its blood is its life; therefore I have said to the people of Israel: You shall not eat the blood of any creature, for the life of every creature is its blood; whoever eats it shall be cut off.
text.Rotherham: for, as for the life of all flesh, the blood thereof, for the life thereof, standeth, therefore have I said unto the sons of Israel—Of the blood of no manner of flesh, shall ye partake, For, the life of all flesh is the blood thereof; whoso partaketh thereof, shall be cut off.

text.NRSV: All persons, citizens or aliens, who eat what dies of itself or what has been torn by wild animals, shall wash their clothes, and bathe themselves in water, and be unclean until the evening; then they shall be clean.
text.Rotherham: And, in the case of any person who eateth that which died of itself or was torn in pieces, whether he be home-born, or a sojourner, then shall he wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the evening, and then be clean.

text.NRSV: But if they do not wash themselves or bathe their body, they shall bear their guilt.
text.Rotherham: But, if he wash them not, and his flesh, he do not bathe, then shall he bear his iniquity.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—I—Yahweh, am your God:—

text.NRSV: You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt, where you lived, and you shall not do as they do in the land of Canaan, to which I am bringing you. You shall not follow their statutes.
text.Rotherham: After the doings of the land of Egypt wherein ye dwelt, shall ye not do,—And after the doings of the land of Canaan whither, I, am bringing you in, shall ye not do, And, in their statutes, shall ye not walk:

text.NRSV: My ordinances you shall observe and my statutes you shall keep, following them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: My regulations, shall ye do, And, my statutes, shall ye observe to walk therein,—I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall keep my statutes and my ordinances; by doing so one shall live: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe my statutes, and my regulations, Which if the son of earth shall do them, Then shall he live in them,—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: None of you shall approach anyone near of kin to uncover nakedness: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: No person whatsoever, unto any of the near kin of his own flesh, shall approach to uncover the parts of shame,—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father, which is the nakedness of your mother; she is your mother, you shall not uncover her nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father, even the shame of thy mother, shalt thou not uncover,—thy mother, she is, thou shalt not uncover her shame.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s wife; it is the nakedness of your father.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father’s wife, shalt thou not uncover,—thy father’s shame, it is.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your sister, your father’s daughter or your mother’s daughter, whether born at home or born abroad.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy sister daughter of thy father or daughter of thy mother, born at home or born abroad, thou shalt not uncover their shame.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your son’s daughter or of your daughter’s daughter, for their nakedness is your own nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of the daughter of thy son or the daughter of thy daughter, thou shalt not uncover their shame,—for, thine own shame, they are.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s wife’s daughter, begotten by your father, since she is your sister.
text.Rotherham: The shame of the daughter of thy father’s wife, born to thy father, she being, thy sister, thou shalt not uncover her shame.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s sister; she is your father’s flesh.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father’s sister, shalt thou not uncover,—thy father’s near of kin, she is.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister, for she is your mother’s flesh.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy mother’s sister, shalt thou not uncover,—for, thy mother’s near of kin, she is.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s brother, that is, you shall not approach his wife; she is your aunt.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father’s brother, shalt thou not uncover unto his wife, shalt thou not approach, thine aunt, she is.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law: she is your son’s wife; you shall not uncover her nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy daughter-in-law, shalt thou not uncover,—thy son’s wife, she is, thou shalt not uncover her shame.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother’s wife; it is your brother’s nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy brother’s wife, shalt thou not uncover,—the shame of thy brother, it is.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of a woman and her daughter, and you shall not take her son’s daughter or her daughter’s daughter to uncover her nakedness; they are your flesh; it is depravity.
text.Rotherham: The shame of a woman, and of her daughter, shalt thou not uncover,—neither the daughter of her son nor the daughter of her daughter, shalt thou take, to uncover her shame, near of kin, they are, wickedness, it is.

text.NRSV: And you shall not take a woman as a rival to her sister, uncovering her nakedness while her sister is still alive.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman unto her sister, shalt thou not take,—to cause rivalry, by uncovering her shame besides her own while she is living.

text.NRSV: You shall not approach a woman to uncover her nakedness while she is in her menstrual uncleanness.
text.Rotherham: And unto a woman during her removal for uncleanness, shalt thou not approach, to uncover her shame.

text.NRSV: You shall not have sexual relations with your kinsman’s wife, and defile yourself with her.
text.Rotherham: And of the wife of thy neighbour, shalt thou not have carnal knowledge,—to commit uncleanness with her.

text.NRSV: You shall not give any of your offspring to sacrifice them to Molech, and so profane the name of your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And none of thy seed, shalt thou deliver up, to cause to pass through to Molech,—that thou profane not the name of thy God, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; it is an abomination.
text.Rotherham: And, with mankind, shalt thou not lie as with womankind,—an abomination, it is.

text.NRSV: You shall not have sexual relations with any animal and defile yourself with it, nor shall any woman give herself to an animal to have sexual relations with it: it is perversion.
text.Rotherham: And of no beast, shalt thou have carnal knowledge to commit uncleanness therewith,—neither shall a woman present herself to a beast to couch down thereto,—confusion, it is.

text.NRSV: Do not defile yourselves in any of these ways, for by all these practices the nations I am casting out before you have defiled themselves.
text.Rotherham: Do not make yourselves unclean in any of these things,—For in all these things, have the nations made themselves unclean, whom, I, am sending out from before you.

text.NRSV: Thus the land became defiled; and I punished it for its iniquity, and the land vomited out its inhabitants.
text.Rotherham: Therefore hath the land become unclean, and I have visited the iniquity thereof upon it,—and the land hath vomited her inhabitants.

text.NRSV: But you shall keep my statutes and my ordinances and commit none of these abominations, either the citizen or the alien who resides among you
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, shall observe my statutes and my regulations, and have nothing to do with any of these abominations,—whether the home-born, or the sojourner that sojourneth in your midst;

text.NRSV: (for the inhabitants of the land, who were before you, committed all of these abominations, and the land became defiled);
text.Rotherham: For all these abominations, have the men of the land done, who were before you; And so the land hath become unclean:

text.NRSV: otherwise the land will vomit you out for defiling it, as it vomited out the nation that was before you.
text.Rotherham: So shall the land not vomit you, through your making it unclean,—as it vomited the nation which was before you.

text.NRSV: For whoever commits any of these abominations shall be cut off from their people.
text.Rotherham: For whosoever shall have anything to do with any of these abominations, the persons who have, shall be cut off, out of the midst of their people.

text.NRSV: So keep my charge not to commit any of these abominations that were done before you, and not to defile yourselves by them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye keep my charge, So that ye may have nothing to do with any of the abominable statutes with which they have had to do before you, So shall ye not make yourselves unclean thereby,—I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to all the congregation of the people of Israel and say to them: You shall be holy, for I the Lord your God am holy.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel and thou shalt say unto them—Holy, shall ye be,—For holy, am I—Yahweh—your God

text.NRSV: You shall each revere your mother and father, and you shall keep my sabbaths: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall, every one, revere, his father and his mother, And my sabbaths, shall ye observe,—I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: Do not turn to idols or make cast images for yourselves: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Do not turn unto things of nought, And, molten gods, shall ye not make to yourselves,—I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: When you offer a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord, offer it in such a way that it is acceptable in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: And when ye would offer a peace-offering unto Yahweh, ye shall offer it that ye may be accepted.

text.NRSV: It shall be eaten on the same day you offer it, or on the next day; and anything left over until the third day shall be consumed in fire.
text.Rotherham: On the day, ye offer it, shall it be eaten, and on the morrow,—but, that which remaineth until the third day, in fire, shall be consumed.

text.NRSV: If it is eaten at all on the third day, it is an abomination; it will not be acceptable.
text.Rotherham: And, if it, be eaten, on the third day, unclean, it is it shall not be accepted,

text.NRSV: All who eat it shall be subject to punishment, because they have profaned what is holy to the Lord; and any such person shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: But he that eateth it, his iniquity, shall bear, because that which had been hallowed unto Yahweh, hath he profaned,—so then that person shall be cut off from among his kinsfolk.

text.NRSV: When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap to the very edges of your field, or gather the gleanings of your harvest.
text.Rotherham: And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly clear the border of thy field, in reaping,—nor shalt thou gather up the gleaning of thy harvest.

text.NRSV: You shall not strip your vineyard bare, or gather the fallen grapes of your vineyard; you shall leave them for the poor and the alien: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And, thy vineyard, shalt thou not go over again, nor gather, every single grape: for the poor and for the sojourner, shalt thou leave them, I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall not steal; you shall not deal falsely; and you shall not lie to one another.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not steal,—nor deceive nor lie one man to another;

text.NRSV: And you shall not swear falsely by my name, profaning the name of your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Nor swear by my name falsely,—And so profane the name of thy God: I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall not defraud your neighbor; you shall not steal; and you shall not keep for yourself the wages of a laborer until morning.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not oppress thy neighbour nor rob [him],—The wages of him that is hired shall not tarry with thee, until the morning.

text.NRSV: You shall not revile the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind; you shall fear your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not curse the deaf, Nor, before the blind, place a stumbling-block,—So shalt thou stand in awe of thy God I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall not render an unjust judgment; you shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great: with justice you shall judge your neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not act perversely in giving judgment, Thou shalt neither respect the person of the poor, Nor prefer the person of the great,—In righteousness, shalt thou judge thy neighbour.

text.NRSV: You shall not go around as a slanderer among your people, and you shall not profit by the blood of your neighbor: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not go about talebearing, among thy people Thou shalt not stand by, over the blood of thy neighbour: I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall not hate in your heart anyone of your kin; you shall reprove your neighbor, or you will incur guilt yourself.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thy heart,—Thou shalt, faithfully reprove, thy neighbour, and not countenance him in sin:

text.NRSV: You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against any of your people, but you shall love your neighbor as yourself: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not take vengeance neither shalt thou cherish anger against the sons of thy people, So shalt thou love thy neighbour as thyself,—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall keep my statutes. You shall not let your animals breed with a different kind; you shall not sow your field with two kinds of seed; nor shall you put on a garment made of two different materials.
text.Rotherham: My statutes, shall ye observe, Thy beasts, shalt thou not cause to breed in two kinds, Thy field, shalt thou not sow with two sorts of seed,—And a garment woven of diverse threads, shalt thou not suffer to come upon thee.

text.NRSV: If a man has sexual relations with a woman who is a slave, designated for another man but not ransomed or given her freedom, an inquiry shall be held. They shall not be put to death, since she has not been freed;
text.Rotherham: And whosoever lieth carnally with a woman, she being a bondmaid, acquired for a husband, and neither, redeemed, nor, freedom, given her, inquisition, shall be made they shall not be put to death, because she was not free;

text.NRSV: but he shall bring a guilt offering for himself to the Lord, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, a ram as guilt offering.
text.Rotherham: but he shall bring in his guilt-bearer unto Yahweh, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting,—even a ram as a guilt-bearer;

text.NRSV: And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of guilt offering before the Lord for his sin that he committed; and the sin he committed shall be forgiven him.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall put a propitiatory-covering over him with the guilt-bearing ram before Yahweh, on account of his sin which he hath committed,—so shall he have forgiveness, from his sin which he hath committed.

text.NRSV: When you come into the land and plant all kinds of trees for food, then you shall regard their fruit as forbidden; three years it shall be forbidden to you, it must not be eaten.
text.Rotherham: And when ye come into the land, and plant any manner of fruit-tree, then shall ye count as uncircumcised the fruit thereof, three years, shall it be to you as if unclean, it shall not be eaten.

text.NRSV: In the fourth year all their fruit shall be set apart for rejoicing in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But, in the fourth year, all the fruit thereof shall be hallowed for a festival of thanksgiving unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: But in the fifth year you may eat of their fruit, that their yield may be increased for you: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And in the fifth year, shall ye eat the fruit thereof, that it may enrich you with its increase,—I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall not eat anything with its blood. You shall not practice augury or witchcraft.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall eat nothing with the blood thereof,—Ye shall not practise divination neither shall ye use magic.

text.NRSV: You shall not round off the hair on your temples or mar the edges of your beard.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not shave in a circle around your head,—nor shalt thou disfigure the fringe of thy beard.

text.NRSV: You shall not make any gashes in your flesh for the dead or tattoo any marks upon you: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Cuttings for a dead person, shall ye not make in your flesh. And punctures in your persons, shall ye not print,—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Do not profane your daughter by making her a prostitute, that the land not fall into whoredom and the land become full of depravity.
text.Rotherham: Do not profane thy daughter by causing her to be unchaste,—lest the land fall to unchastity, and so the land be filled with wickedness.

text.NRSV: You shall keep my sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: My sabbaths, shall ye observe, And my sanctuary, shall ye revere,—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Do not turn to mediums or wizards; do not seek them out, to be defiled by them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Do not turn unto mediums, Nor, for oracles, make search, To render yourselves unclean with them, I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall rise before the aged, and defer to the old; and you shall fear your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Before a hoary head, shalt thou rise up, And shalt honour the presence of an elder,—So shalt thou stand in awe of thy God—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: When an alien resides with you in your land, you shall not oppress the alien.
text.Rotherham: And, when there sojourneth with thee a sojourner in your land, ye shall not oppress him:

text.NRSV: The alien who resides with you shall be to you as the citizen among you; you shall love the alien as yourself, for you were aliens in the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: As one home born from among yourselves, shall be unto you the sojourner who sojourneth with you, So shalt thou love him as thyself, For, sojourners, became ye, in the land of Egypt,—I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall not cheat in measuring length, weight, or quantity.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not act perversely in giving judgment,—in measures of extent, in weights in measures of capacity:

text.NRSV: You shall have honest balances, honest weights, an honest ephah, and an honest hin: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Just balances, just weights, a just ephah and a just hin, shall ye have,—I—Yahweh, am your God, who brought you forth out of the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: You shall keep all my statutes and all my ordinances, and observe them: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes and all my regulations and do them,—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Say further to the people of Israel: Any of the people of Israel, or of the aliens who reside in Israel, who give any of their offspring to Molech shall be put to death; the people of the land shall stone them to death.
text.Rotherham: Unto the sons of Israel, therefore shalt thou say, What man soever, there may be of the sons of Israel, or of the sojourners that sojourn in Israel, that giveth of his seed unto Molech, he shall, surely be put to death,—the people of the land shall stone him with stones;

text.NRSV: I myself will set my face against them, and will cut them off from the people, because they have given of their offspring to Molech, defiling my sanctuary and profaning my holy name.
text.Rotherham: I, also, will set my face against that man, and will cut him off out of the midst of his people,—because of his seed, hath he given unto Molech, seeing that he hath made unclean my sanctuary, even to the extent of profaning my holy name.

text.NRSV: And if the people of the land should ever hide their eyes from those persons, when they give of their offspring to Molech, and do not put them to death,
text.Rotherham: But, if the people of the land, do even hide, their eyes from that man, when he giveth of his seed unto Molech,—so as not to put him to death,

text.NRSV: I myself will set my face against them and against their family, and will cut them off from their people, them and all who follow them in prostituting themselves to Molech.
text.Rotherham: then will I myself, set my face against that man, and against his family,—and will cut him off, and all that follow unchastely after him—in going unchastely after Molech—out of the midst of their people.

text.NRSV: If any turn to mediums and wizards, prostituting themselves to them, I will set my face against them, and will cut them off from the people.
text.Rotherham: The person also that turneth unto mediums and unto oracles, in going unchastely after them, then will I set my face against that person, and will cut him off out of the midst of his people.

text.NRSV: Consecrate yourselves therefore, and be holy; for I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye hallow yourselves and be holy,—Because I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: Keep my statutes, and observe them; I am the Lord; I sanctify you.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe my statutes, and do them,—I—Yahweh, am he that is hallowing you.

text.NRSV: All who curse father or mother shall be put to death; having cursed father or mother, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: When, any man whatsoever, curseth his father or his mother, he shall surely be put to death,—his father or his mother, hath he cursed, his blood, shall be upon himself.

text.NRSV: If a man commits adultery with the wife of his neighbor, both the adulterer and the adulteress shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who committeth adultery with the wife of any other man, he that committeth adultery with the wife of his neighbour shall surely be put to death—the adulterer, and the adulteress.

text.NRSV: The man who lies with his father’s wife has uncovered his father’s nakedness; both of them shall be put to death; their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And any man who lieth with his father’s wife, the shame of his father, hath uncovered,—they both shall, surely be put to death—their blood shall be upon themselves.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with his daughter-in-law, both of them shall be put to death; they have committed perversion, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And any man who lieth with his daughter-in-law, they both shall, surely be put to death, confusion, have they wrought—their blood shall be upon themselves.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with a male as with a woman, both of them have committed an abomination; they shall be put to death; their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And any man who lieth with mankind as with womankind, an abomination, have both of them wrought,—they shall, surely be put to death,—their blood, shall be upon themselves.

text.NRSV: If a man takes a wife and her mother also, it is depravity; they shall be burned to death, both he and they, that there be no depravity among you.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who taketh a woman and her mother, wickedness, it is,—in fire, shall both he and they be consumed, that wickedness be not in your midst.

text.NRSV: If a man has sexual relations with an animal, he shall be put to death; and you shall kill the animal.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who hath carnal knowledge of a beast, shall, surely be put to death,—and, the beast, shall ye slay.

text.NRSV: If a woman approaches any animal and has sexual relations with it, you shall kill the woman and the animal; they shall be put to death, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman, who approacheth unto any beast, to couch down thereto, then shalt thou slay the woman and the beast,—they shall surely be put to death,—their blood, shall be upon themselves.

text.NRSV: If a man takes his sister, a daughter of his father or a daughter of his mother, and sees her nakedness, and she sees his nakedness, it is a disgrace, and they shall be cut off in the sight of their people; he has uncovered his sister’s nakedness, he shall bear his iniquity.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who taketh his sister—his father’s daughter or his mother’s daughter, and vieweth her shame, and, she, vieweth his shame, a disgrace, it is,—they shall therefore be cut off in the sight of the sons of their people,—the shame of his sister, hath he uncovered—his iniquity, shall he bear.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with a woman having her sickness and uncovers her nakedness, he has laid bare her flow and she has laid bare her flow of blood; both of them shall be cut off from their people.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who lieth with a woman having her sickness, and uncovereth her shame, her fountain, hath he exposed, she, also hath uncovered her fountain of blood,—they shall therefore both be cut off out of the midst of their people.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister or of your father’s sister, for that would uncover one’s own flesh; they shall bear their iniquity.
text.Rotherham: And the shame of thy mother’s sister, or of thy father’s sister, shalt thou not uncover,—for his near of kin, hath he exposed—their iniquity, shall they bear.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with his uncle’s wife, he has uncovered his uncle’s nakedness; they shall bear their sin, they shall die childless.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who lieth with his uncle’s wife, the shame of his uncle, hath he uncovered,—their sin, shall they bear—childless, shall they die.

text.NRSV: If a man takes his brother’s wife, it is impurity; he has uncovered his brother’s nakedness; they shall be childless.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who taketh his brother’s wife, impurity, it is,—the shame of his brother, hath he uncovered—childless, shall they remain.

text.NRSV: You shall keep all my statutes and all my ordinances, and observe them, so that the land to which I bring you to settle in does not vomit you out.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes and all my regulations, and do them,—So shall the land whereinto I am bringing you to dwell therein, not vomit you forth;

text.NRSV: You shall not follow the practices of the nation that I am driving out before you. Because they did all these things, I abhorred them.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye not walk in the—statutes of the nations, which I am casting out from before you,—For all these things, had they done, Therefore I abhorred them;

text.NRSV: But I have said to you: You shall inherit their land, and I will give it to you to possess, a land flowing with milk and honey. I am the Lord your God; I have separated you from the peoples.
text.Rotherham: And said unto you—Ye, shall possess their soil, Yea, I myself, will give it you to possess it, A land flowing with milk and honey,—I—Yahweh, am your God, who have distinguished you from the peoples;

text.NRSV: You shall therefore make a distinction between the clean animal and the unclean, and between the unclean bird and the clean; you shall not bring abomination on yourselves by animal or by bird or by anything with which the ground teems, which I have set apart for you to hold unclean.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye make a distinction—Between the clean beasts, and the unclean,—And between the unclean birds and the clean,—So shall ye not make your persons abominable with beast, or with bird or with anything which creepeth upon the ground, which I have distinguished for you, as unclean.

text.NRSV: You shall be holy to me; for I the Lord am holy, and I have separated you from the other peoples to be mine.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye be unto me, holy persons, for, holy, am, I—Yahweh,—Therefore have I distinguished you from the peoples, that ye may be mine.

text.NRSV: A man or a woman who is a medium or a wizard shall be put to death; they shall be stoned to death, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And as touching man or woman, when there shall be in them a familiar spirit, or the spirit of an oracle, they shall, surely be put to death,—with stones, shall they be stoned—their blood, shall be upon themselves.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses: Speak to the priests, the sons of Aaron, and say to them: No one shall defile himself for any dead person among his kin,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses, Say unto the priests, the sons of Aaron,—yea thou shalt say unto them, For a dead person, shall no one make himself unclean among his people:

text.NRSV: except for his nearest kin: his mother, his father, his son, his daughter, his brother;
text.Rotherham: Saving for his kin, that are near unto him, for his mother or for his father, or for his son or for his daughter, or for his brother;

text.NRSV: likewise, for a virgin sister, close to him because she has had no husband, he may defile himself for her.
text.Rotherham: or for his sister, a virgin who is near unto him, who belongeth not unto a husband,—for her, he may make himself unclean:

text.NRSV: But he shall not defile himself as a husband among his people and so profane himself.
text.Rotherham: He shall not make himself unclean being a chief among his people,—by profaning himself:

text.NRSV: They shall not make bald spots upon their heads, or shave off the edges of their beards, or make any gashes in their flesh.
text.Rotherham: They shall not make a baldness behind in their head, And the border of their beard, shall they not cut off,—And, in their flesh, shall they not make incisions.

text.NRSV: They shall be holy to their God, and not profane the name of their God; for they offer the Lord’s offerings by fire, the food of their God, and therefore they shall be holy.
text.Rotherham: Holy persons, shall they be unto their God, and not profane the name of their God,—for, the altar-flames of Yahweh, the food of their God, do they bring near—Therefore shall they be holy.

text.NRSV: They shall not marry a prostitute or a woman who has been defiled; neither shall they marry a woman divorced from her husband. For they are holy to their God,
text.Rotherham: A woman that is unchaste or dishonoured, shall they not take, And a woman divorced from her husband, shall they not take,—For holy, he is unto his God.

text.NRSV: and you shall treat them as holy, since they offer the food of your God; they shall be holy to you, for I the Lord, who sanctify you, am holy.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou hold him as holy, for the food of thy God, doth he bring near,—Holy, shall he be to thee, For, holy, am I—Yahweh, who am making you holy.

text.NRSV: When the daughter of a priest profanes herself through prostitution, she profanes her father; she shall be burned to death.
text.Rotherham: And, the daughter of any priest, when she profaneth herself by unchastity, Her father, she, doth profane, with fire, shall she be consumed.

text.NRSV: The priest who is exalted above his fellows, on whose head the anointing oil has been poured and who has been consecrated to wear the vestments, shall not dishevel his hair, nor tear his vestments.
text.Rotherham: And, as for the high priest from among his brethren upon whose head is poured the anointing oil, and who is installed, by putting on the garments, His head, shall he not bare, And, his garments, shall he not rend;

text.NRSV: He shall not go where there is a dead body; he shall not defile himself even for his father or mother.
text.Rotherham: And, unto no persons of the dead, shall he go in,—For his father or for his mother, shall he not make himself unclean;

text.NRSV: He shall not go outside the sanctuary and thus profane the sanctuary of his God; for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, out of the sanctuary, shall he not go forth, so shall he not profane the sanctuary of his God,—for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: He shall marry only a woman who is a virgin.
text.Rotherham: But, he, shall take a woman in her virginity:

text.NRSV: A widow, or a divorced woman, or a woman who has been defiled, a prostitute, these he shall not marry. He shall marry a virgin of his own kin,
text.Rotherham: A widow or a divorced woman or one dishonoured—an unchaste woman, these, shall he not take, But, a virgin from among his own kinsfolk, shall he take to wife;

text.NRSV: that he may not profane his offspring among his kin; for I am the Lord; I sanctify him.
text.Rotherham: So shall he not profane his seed, among his kinsfolk,—For I—Yahweh, do hallow him.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and say: No one of your offspring throughout their generations who has a blemish may approach to offer the food of his God.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, saying,—No man of thy seed, unto their generations in whom shall be any blemish, shall come near, to present the food of his God.

text.NRSV: For no one who has a blemish shall draw near, one who is blind or lame, or one who has a mutilated face or a limb too long,
text.Rotherham: Surely, no man in whom is any blemish, shall come near,—No man who is blind or lame, or hath a flat nose, or is lanky;

text.NRSV: or one who has a broken foot or a broken hand,
text.Rotherham: nor any man who hath a broken foot,—or a broken hand;

text.NRSV: or a hunchback, or a dwarf, or a man with a blemish in his eyes or an itching disease or scabs or crushed testicles.
text.Rotherham: or is hump-backed or a dwarf, or hath defective vision,—or hath scurvy or scab, or is crushed in the stones:

text.NRSV: No descendant of Aaron the priest who has a blemish shall come near to offer the Lord’s offerings by fire; since he has a blemish, he shall not come near to offer the food of his God.
text.Rotherham: No, man in whom is a blemish of the seed of Aaron the priest, shall approach, to bring near the altar-flames of Yahweh,—a blemish, is in him, the food of his God, shall he not approach to bring near:

text.NRSV: He may eat the food of his God, of the most holy as well as of the holy,
text.Rotherham: Of the food of his God, both of the most holy,—and the holy, may he eat:

text.NRSV: but he shall not come near the curtain or approach the altar, because he has a blemish, that he may not profane my sanctuaries; for I am the Lord; I sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: Only, unto the veil, shall he not come in and unto the altar, shall he not approach—because, a blemish, is in him, so shall he not profane my sanctuaries, For, I—Yahweh, am hallowing them.

text.NRSV: Thus Moses spoke to Aaron and to his sons and to all the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake [these things] unto Aaron, and unto his sons,—and unto all the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Direct Aaron and his sons to deal carefully with the sacred donations of the people of Israel, which they dedicate to me, so that they may not profane my holy name; I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons, That they hold sacredly aloof from the holy things of the sons of Israel, so as not to profane my holy name,—in the things which they are hallowing unto me—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Say to them: If anyone among all your offspring throughout your generations comes near the sacred donations, which the people of Israel dedicate to the Lord, while he is in a state of uncleanness, that person shall be cut off from my presence: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Say unto them—Unto your generations, as touching any man who cometh near from among any of your seed, unto the hallowed things which the sons of Israel may hallow unto Yahweh, while his uncleanness is upon him, then shall that person be cut off from before me—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: No one of Aaron’s offspring who has a leprous disease or suffers a discharge may eat of the sacred donations until he is clean. Whoever touches anything made unclean by a corpse or a man who has had an emission of semen,
text.Rotherham: No man soever of the seed of Aaron who is a leper, or hath a flux, shall eat of the holy things, until he be clean,—And as for him who toucheth anything that is unclean by the dead, or a man whose seed goeth from him;

text.NRSV: and whoever touches any swarming thing by which he may be made unclean or any human being by whom he may be made unclean—whatever his uncleanness may be—
text.Rotherham: or a man who toucheth any creeping thing, which is unclean to him,—or [toucheth] any human being who hath uncleanness, to the extent of any thing that maketh him unclean,

text.NRSV: the person who touches any such shall be unclean until evening and shall not eat of the sacred donations unless he has washed his body in water.
text.Rotherham: any person who toucheth any such, shall then be unclean until the evening,—and shall not eat of the hallowed things, unless he bathe his flesh in water.

text.NRSV: When the sun sets he shall be clean; and afterward he may eat of the sacred donations, for they are his food.
text.Rotherham: When the sun tooth in, then is he clean,—and thereafter, he may eat of the hallowed things, for, his food, it is.

text.NRSV: That which died or was torn by wild animals he shall not eat, becoming unclean by it: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: That which dieth of itself or is torn in pieces, shall he not eat to make himself unclean thereby,—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: They shall keep my charge, so that they may not incur guilt and die in the sanctuary for having profaned it: I am the Lord; I sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall they keep my charge, and shall not bear, for it, sin, and die therein, when they profane it,—I—Yahweh, am hallowing them.

text.NRSV: No lay person shall eat of the sacred donations. No bound or hired servant of the priest shall eat of the sacred donations;
text.Rotherham: And, no stranger, shall eat what is hallowed,—neither, one who dwelleth with a priest nor a hireling shall eat what is hallowed.

text.NRSV: but if a priest acquires anyone by purchase, the person may eat of them; and those that are born in his house may eat of his food.
text.Rotherham: But when, a priest, buyeth any person, as the purchase of his silver, he may eat thereof,—and, the children of his household, they, may eat of his food.

text.NRSV: If a priest’s daughter marries a layman, she shall not eat of the offering of the sacred donations;
text.Rotherham: And, when, a priest’s daughter, belongeth to a husband, who is a stranger, she, of the heave-offering of the hallowed things, may not eat.

text.NRSV: but if a priest’s daughter is widowed or divorced, without offspring, and returns to her father’s house, as in her youth, she may eat of her father’s food. No lay person shall eat of it.
text.Rotherham: But, when a priest’s daughter, cometh to be a widow or divorced and hath no seed, and so she returneth unto the house of her father, as in her youth, of the food of her father, she may eat,—but no stranger, shall eat thereof.

text.NRSV: If a man eats of the sacred donation unintentionally, he shall add one-fifth of its value to it, and give the sacred donation to the priest.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, eateth what is hallowed, by mistake, then shall he add the fifth part of it thereunto, and give unto the priest the hallowed thing:

text.NRSV: No one shall profane the sacred donations of the people of Israel, which they offer to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: So shall they not profane the hallowed things of the sons of Israel,—which they heave up unto Yahweh;

text.NRSV: causing them to bear guilt requiring a guilt offering, by eating their sacred donations: for I am the Lord; I sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: nor cause them to bear guilty iniquity, when they eat their hallowed things,—For, I—Yahweh, am hallowing them.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and his sons and all the people of Israel and say to them: When anyone of the house of Israel or of the aliens residing in Israel presents an offering, whether in payment of a vow or as a freewill offering that is offered to the Lord as a burnt offering,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons, and unto all the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them—What man, soever, of the house of Israel, or of the sojourners in Israel, may bring near his oblation, as regardeth any of their vows, or any of their freewill offerings which they may bring near unto Yahweh, as an ascending-sacrifice,

text.NRSV: to be acceptable in your behalf it shall be a male without blemish, of the cattle or the sheep or the goats.
text.Rotherham: that ye may be accepted, [it must be] a male without defect, of the beeves, or of the sheep, or of the goats:

text.NRSV: You shall not offer anything that has a blemish, for it will not be acceptable in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: whatsoever hath in it a blemish, shall ye not bring near,—for it shall not be accepted for you.

text.NRSV: When anyone offers a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord, in fulfillment of a vow or as a freewill offering, from the herd or from the flock, to be acceptable it must be perfect; there shall be no blemish in it.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever would bring near a peace-offering unto Yahweh, to consecrate a vow or as a freewill-offering with a bullock or a sheep, without defect, shall it be, to be accepted, no, blemish, shall be therein.

text.NRSV: Anything blind, or injured, or maimed, or having a wen, or mangy, or scabbed—you shall not offer to the Lord or put any of them on the altar as offerings by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Blind, or broken, or rent, or having a running sore, or scurvy, or scab, ye shall not bring these near unto Yahweh,—and no, altar-flame, shall ye present therefrom upon the altar, unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: An ox or a lamb that has a limb too long or too short you may present for a freewill offering; but it will not be accepted for a vow.
text.Rotherham: Whether an ox or a lamb long or short limb, as a free-will offering, thou mayest offer it, but, for a vow, it shall not be accepted.

text.NRSV: Any animal that has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn or cut, you shall not offer to the Lord; such you shall not do within your land,
text.Rotherham: But that which is bruised in the stones or broken therein, or torn or cut, shall ye neither bring ear unto Yahweh, nor on your own land, shall ye offer.

text.NRSV: nor shall you accept any such animals from a foreigner to offer as food to your God; since they are mutilated, with a blemish in them, they shall not be accepted in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: Even at the hand of the son of a stranger, shall ye not bring near the food of your God of any of these,—for, their damage is in them, a blemish, is in them, they shall not be accepted for you.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:—

text.NRSV: When an ox or a sheep or a goat is born, it shall remain seven days with its mother, and from the eighth day on it shall be acceptable as the Lord’s offering by fire.
text.Rotherham: When, a beeve or a sheep or a goat, is brought forth, then shall it be seven days under its dam,—and, from the eighth day and onwards, it shall be accepted as an oblation of an altar-flame unto Yahweh,

text.NRSV: But you shall not slaughter, from the herd or the flock, an animal with its young on the same day.
text.Rotherham: Whether cow or ewe—it and its young, shall ye not slaughter on one day.

text.NRSV: When you sacrifice a thanksgiving offering to the Lord, you shall sacrifice it so that it may be acceptable in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: And when ye would offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving unto Yahweh, for your acceptance, shall ye offer it.

text.NRSV: It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall not leave any of it until morning: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: On the same day, shall it be eaten, ye shall not leave thereof until morning,—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Thus you shall keep my commandments and observe them: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall observe my commandments, and do them,—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall not profane my holy name, that I may be sanctified among the people of Israel: I am the Lord; I sanctify you,
text.Rotherham: So shall ye not profane my holy name, So shall I be hallowed in the midst of the sons of Israel,—I, am Yahweh, who am hallowing you;

text.NRSV: I who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Who brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, that I might become your God,—I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: These are the appointed festivals of the Lord that you shall proclaim as holy convocations, my appointed festivals.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel and thou shalt say unto them, As for the appointed seasons of Yahweh, which ye shall proclaim as holy convocations, these, are they—my appointed seasons:—

text.NRSV: Six days shall work be done; but the seventh day is a sabbath of complete rest, a holy convocation; you shall do no work: it is a sabbath to the Lord throughout your settlements.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shall work be done, but, on the seventh day, shall there be a sabbath of sacred rest a holy convocation, no work, shall ye do,—a sabbath, shall it be unto Yahweh, in all your dwellings.

text.NRSV: These are the appointed festivals of the Lord, the holy convocations, which you shall celebrate at the time appointed for them.
text.Rotherham: These, are the appointed seasons of Yahweh, holy convocations,—which ye shall proclaim in their appointed season:—

text.NRSV: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, at twilight, there shall be a passover offering to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: In the first month, on the fourteenth of the month, between the evenings, is a Passover unto Yahweh;

text.NRSV: and on the fifteenth day of the same month is the festival of unleavened bread to the Lord; seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
text.Rotherham: and on the fifteenth day of this month, is the festival of unleavened cakes unto Yahweh,—seven days—unleavened cakes, shall ye eat.

text.NRSV: On the first day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: On the first day—a holy convocation, shall there be to you: no laborious work, shall ye do.

text.NRSV: For seven days you shall present the Lord’s offerings by fire; on the seventh day there shall be a holy convocation: you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh, seven days,—on the seventh day, [shall there be] a holy convocation, no laborious work, shall ye do.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you and you reap its harvest, you shall bring the sheaf of the first fruits of your harvest to the priest.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them:—When ye enter into the land which, I, am giving you, and ye reap the harvest thereof, then shall ye bring in a sheaf of the first-ripe corn of your harvest, unto the priest;

text.NRSV: He shall raise the sheaf before the Lord, that you may find acceptance; on the day after the sabbath the priest shall raise it.
text.Rotherham: and he shall wave the sheaf before Yahweh, that ye may be accepted,—on the morrow of the sabbath, shall the priest wave it.

text.NRSV: On the day when you raise the sheaf, you shall offer a lamb a year old, without blemish, as a burnt offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall offer on the day when ye wave the sheaf, a he-lamb without defect, the finest of its year for an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh;

text.NRSV: And the grain offering with it shall be two-tenths of an ephah of choice flour mixed with oil, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord; and the drink offering with it shall be of wine, one-fourth of a hin.
text.Rotherham: and the, meal-offering thereof shall be two-tenth parts of fine meal overflowed with oil, as an altar-flame unto Yahweh a satisfying odour,—and, the drink-offering thereof, [shall be] mine, the fourth of a hin.

text.NRSV: You shall eat no bread or parched grain or fresh ears until that very day, until you have brought the offering of your God: it is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your settlements.
text.Rotherham: And neither bread, nor roasted corn, nor garden-land grain, shall ye eat, until this selfsame day, until ye have brought in the oblation of your God,—an age-abiding statute unto your generations, in all your dwellings.

text.NRSV: And from the day after the sabbath, from the day on which you bring the sheaf of the elevation offering, you shall count off seven weeks; they shall be complete.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye keep count to yourselves from the morrow of the sabbath, from the day ye brought in the wave sheaf,—seven sabbaths complete, shall there be:

text.NRSV: You shall count until the day after the seventh sabbath, fifty days; then you shall present an offering of new grain to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: until on the morrow of the seventh sabbath, ye count fifty days,—then shall ye bring near a new meal-offering unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall bring from your settlements two loaves of bread as an elevation offering, each made of two-tenths of an ephah; they shall be of choice flour, baked with leaven, as first fruits to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Out of your dwellings, shall ye bring in two wave loaves of two-tenths of an ephah, of fine meal, shall they be, with leaven, shall they be baked,—first-fruits unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall present with the bread seven lambs a year old without blemish, one young bull, and two rams; they shall be a burnt offering to the Lord, along with their grain offering and their drink offerings, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye bring near with the bread seven he-lambs without defect of the first year, and one young bullock, and two rams,—they shall be an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh, with their meal-offering, and their drink-offerings, an altar-flame of satisfying odour unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall also offer one male goat for a sin offering, and two male lambs a year old as a sacrifice of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye offer one he-goat, as a sin-bearer,—and two he-lambs, of the first year, as a peace-offering.

text.NRSV: The priest shall raise them with the bread of the first fruits as an elevation offering before the Lord, together with the two lambs; they shall be holy to the Lord for the priest.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall wave them, with the firstfruits’ bread, as a wave-offering before Yahweh, with the two he-lambs,—holy, shall they be unto Yahweh, for the priest.

text.NRSV: On that same day you shall make proclamation; you shall hold a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations. This is a statute forever in all your settlements throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall make proclamation on this self-same day—a holy convocation, shall it be unto you; no laborious work, shall ye do,—an age-abiding statute in all your dwellings, unto your generations.

text.NRSV: When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap to the very edges of your field, or gather the gleanings of your harvest; you shall leave them for the poor and for the alien: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And, when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly clear the border of thy field when thou reapest, and, the gleanings of thy field, shalt thou not glean,—for the poor and for the sojourner, shalt thou leave them. I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: In the seventh month, on the first day of the month, you shall observe a day of complete rest, a holy convocation commemorated with trumpet blasts.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying:—In the seventh month on the first of the month, shall there be unto you a sacred rest, a trumpet-blowing commemoration a holy convocation:

text.NRSV: You shall not work at your occupations; and you shall present the Lord’s offering by fire.
text.Rotherham: no laborious work, shall ye do,—but ye shall bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying,

text.NRSV: Now, the tenth day of this seventh month is the day of atonement; it shall be a holy convocation for you: you shall deny yourselves and present the Lord’s offering by fire;
text.Rotherham: Surely on the tenth of this seventh month, is, the Day of Propitiation, a holy convocation, shall it be to you, therefore shall ye humble your souls,—and bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: you shall do no work during that entire day; for it is a day of atonement, to make atonement on your behalf before the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And, no work, shall ye do, on this selfsame day,—for a day of propitiation, it is, to put a propitiatory-covering over you before Yahweh your God.

text.NRSV: For any person who is not denied in self on that very day shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever be the person that shall not be humbled on this selfsame day, then shall he be cut off from among his kinsfolk.

text.NRSV: And any person who does any work during that entire day, such a person I will destroy from the midst of the people.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever be the person that shall do any work on this selfsame day, then will I destroy that person from the midst of his people.

text.NRSV: You shall do no work: it is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your settlements.
text.Rotherham: No work, shall ye do,—an age-abiding statute to your generations, in all your dwellings.

text.NRSV: It shall be to you a sabbath of complete rest, and you shall deny yourselves; on the ninth day of the month at evening, from evening to evening you shall keep your sabbath.
text.Rotherham: A sabbath of sacred rest, shall it be unto you, so shall ye humble your souls,—on the ninth of the month in the evening, from evening to evening, shall ye keep your sabbath.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: On the fifteenth day of this seventh month, and lasting seven days, there shall be the festival of booths to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying:—On the fifteenth day of this seventh month, shall be the festival of booths, for seven days, unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The first day shall be a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: On the first day, [shall be] a holy convocation,—no laborious work, shall ye do.

text.NRSV: Seven days you shall present the Lord’s offerings by fire; on the eighth day you shall observe a holy convocation and present the Lord’s offerings by fire; it is a solemn assembly; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: Seven days, shall ye bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh: on the eighth day—a holy convocation, shall there be unto you so shall ye bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh—the closing of the feast, it is, no laborious work, shall ye do.

text.NRSV: These are the appointed festivals of the Lord, which you shall celebrate as times of holy convocation, for presenting to the Lord offerings by fire—burnt offerings and grain offerings, sacrifices and drink offerings, each on its proper day—
text.Rotherham: These, are the appointed seasons of Yahweh, which ye shall proclaim, as holy convocations,—for bringing near as an altar-flame unto Yahweh, an ascending-sacrifice, and a meal-offering, a sacrifice and a drink-offering, each day’s appointment on its own day:—

text.NRSV: apart from the sabbaths of the Lord, and apart from your gifts, and apart from all your votive offerings, and apart from all your freewill offerings, which you give to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: besides the sabbaths of Yahweh, and besides your presents, and besides all your vows, and besides all your freewill offerings which ye shall give unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Now, the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you have gathered in the produce of the land, you shall keep the festival of the Lord, lasting seven days; a complete rest on the first day, and a complete rest on the eighth day.
text.Rotherham: Surely, on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when ye have gathered in the yield of the land, shall ye celebrate the festival of Yahweh seven days,—on the first day, a sacred rest, and, on the eighth day, a sacred rest.

text.NRSV: On the first day you shall take the fruit of majestic trees, branches of palm trees, boughs of leafy trees, and willows of the brook; and you shall rejoice before the Lord your God for seven days.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall take you, on the first day, boughs of goodly trees, branches, of palm-trees, and boughs of thick trees, and poplars of the ravine,—and shall rejoice before Yahweh your God, seven days.

text.NRSV: You shall keep it as a festival to the Lord seven days in the year; you shall keep it in the seventh month as a statute forever throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye celebrate it as a festival unto Yahweh, seven days in the year,—a statute age-abiding, to your generations. In the seventh month, shall ye celebrate it;

text.NRSV: You shall live in booths for seven days; all citizens in Israel shall live in booths,
text.Rotherham: In booths, shall ye dwell seven days;—All the home-born in Israel, shall dwell in booths:

text.NRSV: so that your generations may know that I made the people of Israel live in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: To the end that your generations may know, that, in booths, made I the sons of Israel to dwell, when I brought them forth out of the land of Egypt,—I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: Thus Moses declared to the people of Israel the appointed festivals of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses declared, the appointed seasons of Yahweh, unto the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Command the people of Israel to bring you pure oil of beaten olives for the lamp, that a light may be kept burning regularly.
text.Rotherham: Command the sons of Israel, That they bring unto thee—pure oil olive, beaten, for giving light,—to cause the lamp to burn up continually.

text.NRSV: Aaron shall set it up in the tent of meeting, outside the curtain of the covenant, to burn from evening to morning before the Lord regularly; it shall be a statute forever throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: Outside the veil of the testimony, in the tent of meeting, shall Aaron order it, from evening until morning, before Yahweh continually,—a statute age-abiding, to your generations.

text.NRSV: He shall set up the lamps on the lampstand of pure gold before the Lord regularly.
text.Rotherham: Upon the pure lampstand, shall he order the lamps,—before Yahweh, continually.

text.NRSV: You shall take choice flour, and bake twelve loaves of it; two-tenths of an ephah shall be in each loaf.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt take fine meal, and bake it, in twelve cakes,—of two-tenths, shall each cake be.

text.NRSV: You shall place them in two rows, six in a row, on the table of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt set them in two rows, six in a row,—upon the pure table, before Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You shall put pure frankincense with each row, to be a token offering for the bread, as an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put upon each row pure frankincense,—so shall it belong unto the bread as a memorial, an altar-flame unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Every sabbath day Aaron shall set them in order before the Lord regularly as a commitment of the people of Israel, as a covenant forever.
text.Rotherham: Sabbath day by sabbath day, shall he order it before Yahweh continually,—from the sons of Israel as an age-abiding covenant:

text.NRSV: They shall be for Aaron and his descendants, who shall eat them in a holy place, for they are most holy portions for him from the offerings by fire to the Lord, a perpetual due.
text.Rotherham: so shall it be for Aaron and for his sons, and they shall eat it in a holy place,—for most holy, shall it be unto him from among the altar-flames of Yahweh, a statute age-abiding.

text.NRSV: A man whose mother was an Israelite and whose father was an Egyptian came out among the people of Israel; and the Israelite woman’s son and a certain Israelite began fighting in the camp.
text.Rotherham: And forth went the son of a woman of Israel—he being the son of a man of Egypt—in the midst of the sons of Israel;

text.NRSV: The Israelite woman’s son blasphemed the Name in a curse. And they brought him to Moses—his mother’s name was Shelomith daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan—
text.Rotherham: and the son of the woman of Israel, and a man of Israel, reviled one another in the camp. And the son of the Israelitish woman uttered contemptuously the Name, and reviled. So they brought him in unto Moses,—now, the name of his mother, was Shelomith, daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan;

text.NRSV: and they put him in custody, until the decision of the Lord should be made clear to them.
text.Rotherham: and they put him in ward,—that a clear answer might be given to them at the mouth of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Take the blasphemer outside the camp; and let all who were within hearing lay their hands on his head, and let the whole congregation stone him.
text.Rotherham: Bring forth him that reviled unto the outside of the camp, then shall all that heard [him] lean their hands upon his head,—and all the assembly shall stone him.

text.NRSV: And speak to the people of Israel, saying: Anyone who curses God shall bear the sin.
text.Rotherham: And, unto the sons of Israel, shalt thou speak saying,—What man soever curseth his God shall bear his sin.

text.NRSV: One who blasphemes the name of the Lord shall be put to death; the whole congregation shall stone the blasphemer. Aliens as well as citizens, when they blaspheme the Name, shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, he that contemptuously uttereth the name of Yahweh, shall be, surely put to death, all the assembly shall, surely stone, him,—as the sojourner so the home-born, when he contemptuously uttereth the Name, he shall be put to death.

text.NRSV: Anyone who kills a human being shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, by smiting taketh the life of any human being, he shall be surely put to death,

text.NRSV: Anyone who kills an animal shall make restitution for it, life for life.
text.Rotherham: And he that by smiting taketh away the life of a beast, shall make it good,—life for life.

text.NRSV: Anyone who maims another shall suffer the same injury in return:
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, inflicteth a blemish upon his neighbour, as he hath done, so, shall it be done to him:

text.NRSV: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth; the injury inflicted is the injury to be suffered.
text.Rotherham: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, as he inflicteth a blemish upon a human being, so, shall one be inflicted upon him.

text.NRSV: One who kills an animal shall make restitution for it; but one who kills a human being shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And he that killeth a beast, shall make it good,-and he that killeth a human being, shall be put to death.

text.NRSV: You shall have one law for the alien and for the citizen: for I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: One rule, shall ye have, as the sojourner, so the home-born, shall be,—For, I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: Moses spoke thus to the people of Israel; and they took the blasphemer outside the camp, and stoned him to death. The people of Israel did as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses spake unto the sons of Israel, and they took forth the reviler, unto the outside of the camp, and stoned him with stones. Thus, the sons of Israel did, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses in Mount Sinai, saying—

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you, the land shall observe a sabbath for the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them:—When ye enter into the land which, I, am giving you, then shall the land keep a sabbath unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Six years you shall sow your field, and six years you shall prune your vineyard, and gather in their yield;
text.Rotherham: Six years, shalt thou sow thy field, and, six years, shalt thou prune thy vineyard,—and gather the increase thereof;

text.NRSV: but in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of complete rest for the land, a sabbath for the Lord: you shall not sow your field or prune your vineyard.
text.Rotherham: but, in the seventh year—a sabbath of sacred rest, shall there be unto the land, a sabbath unto Yahweh: thy field, shalt thou not sow, and, thy vineyard, shalt thou not prune;

text.NRSV: You shall not reap the aftergrowth of your harvest or gather the grapes of your unpruned vine: it shall be a year of complete rest for the land.
text.Rotherham: that which groweth of itself of thy harvest, shalt thou not reap; and the grapes of thine unpruned vines, shalt thou not cut off: a year of sacred rest, shall there be to the land.

text.NRSV: You may eat what the land yields during its sabbath—you, your male and female slaves, your hired and your bound laborers who live with you;
text.Rotherham: So shall the sabbath of the land be unto you for food: unto thee, and unto thy servant and unto thy handmaid,—and unto thy hireling, and unto thy settlers that are sojourning with thee;

text.NRSV: for your livestock also, and for the wild animals in your land all its yield shall be for food.
text.Rotherham: and unto thy tame-beasts, and unto the wild-beasts that are in thy land, shall belong all the increase thereof for food.

text.NRSV: You shall count off seven weeks of years, seven times seven years, so that the period of seven weeks of years gives forty-nine years.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt count to thee seven weeks of years, seven years, seven times,—so shall the days of the seven weeks of years become to thee forty-nine years.

text.NRSV: Then you shall have the trumpet sounded loud; on the tenth day of the seventh month—on the day of atonement—you shall have the trumpet sounded throughout all your land
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou cause a signal-horn to pass through in the seventh month, on the tenth of the month: on the Day of Propitiation, shall ye cause a horn to pass throughout all your land.

text.NRSV: and you shall hallow the fiftieth year and you shall proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you: you shall return every one of you to your property and every one of you to your family.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye hallow the fiftieth year, and proclaim freedom throughout the land to all the dwellers thereof,—a jubilee, shall it be unto you, and ye shall return, every man unto his possession, and every man unto his family, shall ye return.

text.NRSV: That fiftieth year shall be a jubilee for you: you shall not sow, or reap the aftergrowth, or harvest the unpruned vines.
text.Rotherham: A jubilee, shall that fiftieth year be unto you,—ye shall not sow, neither shall ye reap the self-grown corn thereof, nor cut off the grapes of the unpruned vines thereof.

text.NRSV: For it is a jubilee; it shall be holy to you: you shall eat only what the field itself produces.
text.Rotherham: For, a jubilee, it is, holy, shall it be unto you,—out of the field, shall ye eat her increase.

text.NRSV: In this year of jubilee you shall return every one of you to your property.
text.Rotherham: In this same jubilee year, shall ye return every man unto his possession.

text.NRSV: When you make a sale to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor, you shall not cheat one another.
text.Rotherham: And when ye sell anything to thy neighbour, or buy aught at thy neighbour’s hand, do not overreach one another.

text.NRSV: When you buy from your neighbor, you shall pay only for the number of years since the jubilee; the seller shall charge you only for the remaining crop years.
text.Rotherham: By the number of years after the jubilee, shalt thou buy of thy neighbour,—by the number of the years of increase, shall he sell unto thee;

text.NRSV: If the years are more, you shall increase the price, and if the years are fewer, you shall diminish the price; for it is a certain number of harvests that are being sold to you.
text.Rotherham: according to the multitude of the years, shalt thou increase the price thereof, and, according to the fewness of the years, shalt thou diminish the price thereof,—because the sum of the increase, it is that he selleth thee.

text.NRSV: You shall not cheat one another, but you shall fear your God; for I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall not overreach one another; but thou shalt stand in awe of thy God,—for, I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall observe my statutes and faithfully keep my ordinances, so that you may live on the land securely.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore ye shall do my statutes, and my regulations, shall ye observe and do them,—so shall ye dwell upon the land with confidence;

text.NRSV: The land will yield its fruit, and you will eat your fill and live on it securely.
text.Rotherham: and the land shall yield her fruit; and ye shall eat to the full,—and shall dwell with confidence thereupon.

text.NRSV: Should you ask, “What shall we eat in the seventh year, if we may not sow or gather in our crop?”
text.Rotherham: And since ye may say, What shall we eat in the seventh year? Lo! we are not to sow, neither are we to gather our increase!

text.NRSV: I will order my blessing for you in the sixth year, so that it will yield a crop for three years.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I command my blessing upon you, in the sixth year,—and it shall make the increase of three years;

text.NRSV: When you sow in the eighth year, you will be eating from the old yield; until the ninth year, when its produce comes in, you shall eat the old.
text.Rotherham: and ye shall sow, the eighth year, and eat of old store,—until the ninth year until the coming in of the increase thereof, shall ye eat old store.
